candidates.direction_name: Restore A Compact Two-Stage PTX Ring While Keeping Grouped Rows At 2
ranking_notes: This is the best deconfounding move because the latest loss is clearly a register-budget failure, not a barrier failure.
Keeping grouped_rows=2 makes the next measurement informative instead of just replaying the exact old grouped_rows=4 anchor.
If this still loses, the data cleanly justifies falling back to the full grouped_rows=4 anchor next.
candidates.hypothesis: The latest regression is dominated by register-pressure and occupancy collapse from the duplicated 3-stage split loop, not by grouped_rows=2 itself. Dropping back to a compact two-stage PTX ring while keeping grouped_rows=2 should recover a large chunk of the 57-register spike and tell us whether the row-group retune can help once the hot path is lean again.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Registers-per-thread and occupancy are the first bottlenecks to remove; barrier cleanup is no longer the primary limiter after the round-6 regression.
candidates.risk: Low to moderate. This stays on the active PTX dispatch path, but it rewrites stage-count and drain control together and still has correctness risk if the two-stage wait cadence is restored incorrectly.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if recovering the two-stage ring requires reopening launch bounds, dispatch routing, or any multi-family rewrite outside the PTX hot-band kernel body.
expected_local_changes: Reduce the PTX hot-band ring from 3 stages back to 2.
Remove the split steady-state and drain loop duplication that inflated the live register set.
Keep kFixedHotBandPtxGroupedRows at 2 during the first two-stage reset.

candidates.direction_name: Restore The Known Two-Stage PTX Anchor With Grouped Rows Back At 4
ranking_notes: This remains the safest fallback because the two-stage grouped_rows=4 anchor is already measured and understood.
It ranks second only because replaying the exact old anchor teaches less than the grouped_rows=2 retest on the lean surface.
If dir_01 is blocked or noisy, this is the immediate recovery branch.
candidates.hypothesis: If the grouped_rows=2 retest still carries avoidable launch-order cost, the clean fallback is to restore the already remeasured two-stage PTX anchor with grouped_rows=4. That anchor is the lowest-variance recent PTX surface and should remove both the extra stage and the row-group retune at once.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: This is a restore family aimed at removing register and occupancy damage rather than discovering a new local bottleneck.
candidates.risk: Low. This is the cleanest rollback path in the current family, though it likely offers less new information than the grouped_rows=2 retest.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the restore requires touching non-PTX dispatch code or reintroducing broader family edits outside the hot-band kernel.
expected_local_changes: Reduce the PTX hot-band ring from 3 stages back to 2.
Return the PTX hot-band loop to the compact two-stage anchor shape.

candidates.direction_name: Reopen The 256x128 64x64-Warp Hot-Band Branch After Resetting Register Pressure
ranking_notes: This remains the highest-ceiling structural branch once the PTX surface is back under control.
The repo already contains the writer-safe 256x128 path, so this is a reopen rather than a greenfield rewrite.
It stays rank 3 because the immediate problem is the round-6 register explosion, not the long-term geometry ceiling.
candidates.hypothesis: Even the cleaner two-stage PTX family stays around 25 ms with only about 16.6% active warps. The persistent human guidance still points at a 256x128 CTA with 64x64 warp tiling and Right-Left-Right-Left reuse as the highest-ceiling structural branch, so it should remain queued once the branch is back on a sane register budget.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: The 128x128 PTX surface may still be capped by geometry and warp-reuse limits even after register cleanup.
candidates.risk: High. This is a broader geometry branch that touches dispatch, ownership, and the writer-safe export path together.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the reopen requires replaying a previously broken ownership or export path instead of the writer-safe 256x128 surface already in the tree.
expected_local_changes: Re-enable the 256x128 / 64x64 PTX hot-band branch on the writer-safe ownership path.
Keep wide async-copy staging and shared-memory reuse intact while reopening the larger CTA geometry.
Leave the non-hot-band and export glue unchanged in the first pass.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
